text.title: Deprecated Api Sweeper Recipe Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of Deprecated Api Sweeper Recipe must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Every step is dry-run before its first real execution. Dry-run artefact (output capture) is stored with the runbook version.
Each step has an explicit success signal (command exit, dashboard delta, alert silence). Steps without success signals are rejected.
rule.rationale: 'check it works' is not a step.

rule.statement: Rollback path is written before forward path. A runbook without a documented rollback is incomplete.
Apply the recipe to one cluster + chart-set per pass. Cross-cluster sweeps MUST be split.
rule.rationale: Anti-conflation pattern: batched sweeps lose per-cluster traceability and let team A's silence mask team B's regression.

rule.statement: Each finding in the report cites: (a) source artefact (cluster name + chart path), (b) tool that flagged it (pluto / kubent), (c) replacement API. Findings without all three are rejected.
rule.rationale: Audit-trail discipline: untraceable findings cannot be acted on after the upgrade window closes.

example.title: Anchored application
example.code: Trigger: Kubernetes major-version upgrade (3 weeks) Artefact: structured output meeting the contract in content/02-output-contract.xml Evidence: ≥1 named source per rule (URL, ticket, transcript_id) Decision: downstream consumer can act without re-deriving reasoning
example.note: Demonstrates the closed loop the methodology creates — trigger → artefact → decision — with auditability at each step.

example.title: Un-anchored application
example.code: Trigger: 'we should do this thing' Artefact: prose paragraph, no schema Evidence: 'industry best practice' Decision: ambiguous — every reader interprets differently
example.note: Each ambiguity above is a failure mode catalogued in content/03-failure-modes.xml. The methodology exists to prevent exactly this shape.
